output:
Reviewing story `21-1-extract-workflow-actors` against the actual code and tests first. I’m locating the story artifacts, reading the extracted modules, and checking whether this stayed a pure extraction with AC coverage intact.The extraction is present, and there are local modifications around verification/proof files. I’m reading the story spec, the extracted modules, and the dispatch tests to verify whether the move stayed behavior-neutral and whether the contract metadata still uses the right inputs.I’ve confirmed the extracted module is small and respects the no-circular-import rule, but I’m checking whether the behavior stayed identical in epic-level verify flows. The likely risk area is how `storyFiles` is now reused for both isolated workspace inputs and contract AC derivation.The current tests exercise isolated workspaces, but I haven’t found a test that checks epic-level `verify` still writes AC metadata from the real story specs. I’m comparing the extracted dispatch path with the old inlined version to see if the extraction introduced a behavior change there.<verdict>fail</verdict>

1. High: Epic-level isolated tasks now derive contract AC metadata from generated guide files instead of the actual story specs, which is a behavior change and breaks AC coverage tracking. In [`collectGuideFiles()`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L559), epic verify/document flows build `guideFiles` from `document-*.json` and `deploy-*.json`, then [`epicStepActor()`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L787) passes those paths as `storyFiles` into [`dispatchTaskCore()`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L82). The extracted contract helper then feeds that same array into [`getPendingAcceptanceCriteria()`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L182), and [`getPendingAcceptanceCriteria()`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-contracts.ts#L5) parses every entry as if it were a story markdown file. Those guide files are user docs / deploy reports, not story specs, so `parseStoryACs()` will usually return no ACs or the wrong ACs. That violates the story’s “pure extraction, zero behavior change” requirement and leaves verify/review contracts with incorrect AC metadata for epic runs.

<issues>
Epic isolated-task contract AC metadata is sourced from `guideFiles` rather than the real story files, causing empty or incorrect `acceptanceCriteria` in epic-level verify/review/document flows. See [`workflow-machine.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L559), [`workflow-machine.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts#L787), [`workflow-actors.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L182), and [`workflow-contracts.ts`](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-contracts.ts#L5).
</issues>

I did not find a separate security issue in the extracted actor module itself. I ran the focused `workflow-machine` test file; it passes, but there is no test covering this epic-level `source_access=false` AC-metadata path, which is why this regression slipped through.
